mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate signifies a robust labor market, which underpins strong consumer confidence and spending, thereby supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods where the UNRATE is below 4.5% and has not increased by more than 0.2 percentage points in the last 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate increases significantly above a critical level, it signals an aggressive monetary tightening stance by the central bank, which typically constrains credit, dampens economic growth, and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.5pp in 3 months AND FEDFUNDS > 2.5%
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of triggered instances where FEDFUNDS rises > 0.5pp in 3 months and is above 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong growth in retail sales and non-farm payrolls indicates robust consumer demand and a healthy employment environment, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and profits, driving S&P 500 forward returns positively.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 0.5% MoM AND PAYEMS growth > 100k MoM
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of periods where both RSAFS growth exceeds 0.5% MoM and PAYEMS growth exceeds 100k MoM.

mechanism_narrative: A substantial decline in housing starts, particularly when accompanied by rising interest rates, indicates a significant weakening in housing demand and broader economic activity, which negatively impacts equity valuations and S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of triggered instances during a rising interest rate environment, defined as FEDFUNDS increasing by >0.25% over 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects an expanding manufacturing sector and overall economic output, which generally leads to improved corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over short horizons.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of periods where INDPRO shows positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: High and accelerating consumer price inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes real purchasing power and corporate profit margins, prompting central bank tightening and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as discount rates rise.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL annual growth > 3.0% AND CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth > 4.0%
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 40% of triggered instances despite high and accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL annual growth > 3.0% and 3-month annualized growth > 4.0%).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of a very low unemployment rate and a high federal funds rate signifies a tight labor market alongside restrictive monetary policy, which can lead to unsustainable wage inflation and an eventual slowdown in economic growth, negatively impacting equity valuations.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of periods where UNRATE is below 4.0% and FEDFUNDS is above 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Robust GDP growth during a period of contained inflation indicates a healthy, non-overheating economy with sustainable expansion, providing a supportive backdrop for corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of periods with GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth > 2.0% and CPIAUCSL annual growth < 2.5%.
